provenance.author: Gemini Code Assist
provenance.context: A semantic capsule describing the Wright Universe memory architecture.

intent.primary_goal: To define the multi-layered memory architecture for the Wright Universe, detailing how memory is partitioned, stored, and accessed by agents and realms.

identity.role: The canonical specification for the distributed memory system of the Wright Universe.

priorities: Memory is partitioned into four scopes: Agent Memory (private), Realm Memory (shared within a world), Project Memory (task-specific), and Universal Knowledge (read-only).
PostgreSQL with pgvector is the standard storage backend for persistent, searchable memory.
Memory access is governed by strict permissions; agents cannot access the private memory of other agents.
The Leighton Loop critiques outcomes and enriches memory capsules with learning signals.
All memory creation and updates must be auditable via ChronoSCRIBE.

file_scope.purpose: To provide a technical blueprint for the project's memory system, ensuring it is scalable, secure, and effective.

key_concepts.agent_memory: An agent's private log of actions, thoughts, and learned heuristics.
key_concepts.realm_memory: A shared memory pool for all agents within a single realm, like Wrightrealm.
key_concepts.project_memory: Context-specific memory tied to a particular building project.
key_concepts.universal_knowledge: Read-only, canonical knowledge derived from foundational capsules.
key_concepts.semantic_retrieval: Using vector similarity search to find memories based on meaning, not just keywords.

trust.note: This capsule is the authoritative technical specification for the project's memory system.
